title: Object
excerpt: An object is a discrete thing in the game world that can occupy space, be interacted with, be targeted, or be affected by rules and effects without being a creature.
markdown:
# Object

An object is a discrete thing in the game world that is not being resolved as a creature. Doors, weapons, chests, levers, vehicles, hazards, furnishings, tools, and many environmental features are treated as objects when rules need to determine targeting, interaction, damage, or movement around them.

## Rules Use

### Distinct From Creatures
- An object is resolved through its physical presence, properties, and current state rather than as a living actor
- A creature can carry, wear, control, create, or interact with an object, but that does not make the object a creature
- Some effects can affect creatures and objects differently, or affect only one of those categories

### Targeting And Effects
- A rule can target an object directly if the effect is written to affect objects, unattended items, structures, or physical features in the environment
- An object can also be caught inside an [[area_of_effect|area of effect]] even when it was not selected individually
- Whether the object is damaged, moved, ignited, blocked, opened, protected, or transformed depends on the effect's own rules

### Interaction State
- Objects often matter because they can be opened, activated, carried, dropped, broken, locked, destroyed, or otherwise changed
- World-state changes to an object can alter movement, line use, cover, hazards, access, or the validity of later actions
- Some objects are simple props, while others are mechanically important because rules explicitly care about them

## Common Uses

- A lever, door, or switch being used through [[interacting_with_objects|object interaction]]
- A chest, weapon, or tool being carried, dropped, or manipulated
- A structure or item being targeted by a spell or damaged by an effect
- Environmental features that occupy space and change how combat or movement resolves
